bibliographic_data.title: Convolutional Neural Generative Coding: Scaling Predictive Coding to Natural Images
bibliographic_data.abstract: In this work, we develop convolutional neural generative coding (Conv-NGC), a generalization of predictive coding to the case of convolution/deconvolution-based computation. Specifically, we concretely implement a flexible neurobiologically-motivated algorithm that progressively refines latent state feature maps in order to dynamically form a more accurate internal representation/reconstruction model of natural images. The performance of the resulting sensory processing system is evaluated on complex datasets such as Color-MNIST, CIFAR-10, and Street House View Numbers (SVHN). We study the effectiveness of our brain-inspired model on the tasks of reconstruction and image denoising and find that it is competitive with convolutional auto-encoding systems trained by backpropagation of errors and outperforms them with respect to out-of-distribution reconstruction (including the full 90k CINIC-10 test set).

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes Convolutional Neural Generative Coding (Conv-NGC), a brain-inspired model that generalizes predictive coding to convolution/deconvolution-based computation, and demonstrates its effectiveness in image reconstruction and denoising tasks.
core_content.key_methodology: Conv-NGC uses a flexible neurobiologically-motivated algorithm that progressively refines latent state feature maps to form an accurate internal representation of natural images. It incorporates (de)convolutional blocks within the framework of predictive coding.
outcomes: Competitive performance with backpropagation-based models on image reconstruction and denoising tasks
Outperforms existing models in out-of-distribution reconstruction
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, we introduce Convolutional Neural Generative Coding (Conv-NGC), a model that generalizes predictive coding to convolution/deconvolution-based computation. The algorithm progressively refines latent state feature maps to form an accurate internal representation of natural images. We evaluate the performance on complex datasets such as Color-MNIST, CIFAR-10, and Street House View Numbers (SVHN). Our results show that Conv-NGC is competitive with backpropagation-based models in tasks like image reconstruction and denoising, and outperforms them in out-of-distribution reconstruction. The model leverages local information similar to how the brain processes visual inputs, making it a promising approach for developing more flexible and energy-efficient neural architectures.
